perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall continue in effect for a period of one year from the date of its execution and from year to year thereafter provided such continuance is specifically approved by a majority of the trustees of the Trust who (i) are not "interested persons" of the Trust or any other party to this Agreement, as defined in the 1940 Act, and (ii) have no direct or indirect financial interest in the operation of this Agreement ("Non- Interested Trustees"). This Agreement will terminate upon the termination of the Advisory Agreement except the Investment Adviser's Right of Recovery set forth in section 1.5 herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will terminate upon the termination of the Advisory Agreement except the Investment Adviser's Right of Recovery set forth in section 1.5 herein. This Agreement shall continue in effect for a period of one year from the date of its execution and from year to year thereafter provided such continuance is specifically approved by a majority of the trustees of the Trust who (i) are not "interested persons" of the Trust or any other party to this Agreement, as defined in the 1940 Act, and (ii) have no direct or indirect financial interest in the operation of this Agreement ("Non- Interested Trustees").
perturbation.explanation: The reordering of the sentences regarding agreement termination and continuation creates ambiguity. It first states the agreement terminates upon advisory agreement termination, and then describes conditions for its annual continuation, creating a logical contradiction. One part says it terminates, the other allows it to continue, making enforcement unclear.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 15(c) requires clarity and proper trustee approval regarding advisory contracts, and this perturbation obscures the conditions for the agreement's continuation/termination, making it harder to ascertain if the trustee approval requirements are being met.

(1/4/1995) Back to Original Document << Previous TITLE 15 / CHAPTER 2D / SUBCHAPTER I / § 80a-15 Next >> [Print] [Print selection] [OLRC Home] Help 15 USC 80a-15 : Contracts of advisers and underwriters Text contains those laws in effect on June 14, 2025 From Title 15-COMMERCE AND TRADE CHAPTER 2D-INVESTMENT COMPANIES AND ADVISERS SUBCHAPTER I-INVESTMENT COMPANIES Jump To: Source Credit Miscellaneous Amendments Effective Date §80a–15. Contracts of advisers and underwriters (a) Written contract to serve or act as investment adviser; contents It shall be unlawful for any person to serve or act as investment adviser of a registered investment company, except pursuant to a written contract, which contract, whether with such registered company or with an investment adviser of such registered company, has
perturbation.justification: The modified text presents a structural flaw that creates a legal contradiction. The reordering of sentences creates ambiguity around the agreement's termination and continuation. The original text clearly defines the agreement's term and conditions for renewal, and only then provides for the circumstances of termination. In contrast, the changed text begins by stating the agreement's termination condition, then adds conditions for yearly continuation, creating a confusing order. This contradicts the clarity requirements for advisory contracts, as described in the Law Explanation, making trustee approval harder to ascertain. Section 15(c) of the Investment Company Act of 1940, as referenced by the Law Explanation, necessitates clarity regarding advisory contracts, and this ambiguity undermines that clarity. The ambiguity makes the text structurally flawed.

perturbation.original_text: 1.7 Operating Expense Limit. Any Expense Waiver recovery payment by the Fund to the Investment Adviser shall not cause the Operating Expense for the Fund's full year of operations to exceed 1.25% of the Fund's average daily net assets. 2. The Underwriter. 3. Term and Termination of Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. The Underwriter. 1.7 Operating Expense Limit. Any Expense Waiver recovery payment by the Fund to the Investment Adviser shall not cause the Operating Expense for the Fund's full year of operations to exceed 1.25% of the Fund's average daily net assets. 3. Term and Termination of Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By relocating the 'Operating Expense Limit' clause to between sections regarding 'The Underwriter' and 'Term and Termination of Agreement,' we obscure its direct relationship to 'Net Investment Income Maintenance' (Section 1). This separation diminishes its prominence and could lead to its oversight, affecting compliance with expense limitations.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 35d-1 necessitates funds to not adopt names suggesting guarantees or investment returns without accurately reflecting the fund's investment policies and risk. Displacing the operating expense limit makes it less visible, obscuring clarity in relation to the fund's performance, potentially misleading investors about expected returns and contradicting the rule.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 270.35d-1 - Investment company names. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 270.35d-1 - Investment company names. CFR prev | next § 270.35d-1 Investment company names. (a) Materially deceptive and misleading fund names. For purposes of section 35(d) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 80a-34(d) ), a materially deceptive and misleading name of a fund includes: (1) Names suggesting guarantee or approval by the United States Government. A name suggesting that the fund or the securities issued by it are guaranteed, sponsored, recommended, or approved by the United States Government or any United States Government agency or instrumentality, including any name that uses the words “guaranteed” or “insured” or similar terms in conjunction with the words “United States” or “U.S. Government.” (2) Names suggesting an investment focus. A name that includes terms suggesting that the fund focuses its investments in: a particular type of investment or investments; a particular industry or group of industries; particular countries or geographic regions; or investments that have, or whose issuers have, particular characteristics ( e.g., a name with terms such as “growth” or “value,” or terms indicating that the fund 's investment decisions incorporate one or more environmental, social, or governance factors), unless: (i) The fund has adopted a policy to invest, under normal circumstances, at least 80% of the value of its assets in investments in accordance with the investment focus that the fund 's name suggests. For a name suggesting that the fund focuses its investments in a particular country or geographic region, investments that are in accordance with the investment focus that the fund 's name suggests are investments that are tied economically to the particular country or geographic region suggested by its name; (ii) The policy described in paragraph (a)(2)(i) of this sec
perturbation.justification: The relocation of the 'Operating Expense Limit' clause, as described in the Perturbation Explanation, weakens its prominence and could lead to its oversight. The Law Explanation states that Rule 35d-1 necessitates funds to not adopt names suggesting guarantees or investment returns without accurately reflecting the fund's investment policies and risk. Scraped Law Snippet 2, which references 17 CFR § 270.35d-1, pertains to materially deceptive and misleading fund names. While Snippet 2 focuses on fund names, the Law Explanation extends its principles to clarity regarding fund performance and investor expectations. By obscuring the operating expense limit, the changed text potentially misleads investors, thereby creating a contradiction under the principles of Rule 35d-1 as it relates to investment company names and misleading information.
